html:
Exclusive to:  

The Portable Crock Pot is a character-specific Item that Warly begins the game with. It acts as a Crock Pot that can be moved and replaced. It must be placed on the ground to cook anything.

While cooking, the Portable Crock Pot gives just enough light to ward off Charlie, but the sanity drain is equivalent of staying in total darkness, therefore, this is not recommended. It can also make exclusive recipes.

Portable Crock Pot exclusive recipes

Main article: Cooking#Crock_Pot

Visual Reminder
Vegetables Meats Fruits Fishes Eggs
Sweeteners Monster Foods Dairy () Bugs () Inedible

Below is a table of the exclusive recipes:

Food Dish Recipe Special Effects
Ingredients Notes
Fresh Fruit Crepes 150 15 60 10d 40s 30 ×1.5×1 ×1 N/A
Monster Tartare 37.5 10 3 10d 40s 30 ×2×1 ×0.5 No N/A
Mussel Bouillabaise 37.5 15 20 10d 40s 30 ×2 ×2.0 N/A
Sweet Potato Souffle 37.5 15 20 10d 40s 30 ×2 ×2.0 N/A

Trivia

  • The Portable Crockpot was exclusive to the Shipwrecked DLC, until it was introduced to Don't Starve Together in the Warly Character Update.

Bugs

  • In Shipwrecked, if the player places a Portable Crock Pot inside a Cargo Boat or Chester, exits the game and then relogs, a second Portable Crock Pot will appear in the player's inventory as the game considers the original one to be missing.
    • The same bug will occur when the player enters a Cave without the Portable Crock Pot in the inventory. Relogging is not necessary for this to occur.

Assets

  • place
  • hit
  • hit full
  • hit cooking
  • cooking
  • collapse

Gallery

  • A deployed Portable Crock Pot.
  • Warly standing next to a Portable Crock Pot.
  • A Butter Muffin made on a Portable Crock Pot.
  • A Portable Crock Pot in action.
  • Place option when hovering the Crock Pot over valid ground.
  • The Portable Crock Pot as seen in the inventory.
  • Faint light from a cooking Portable Crock Pot warding off Charlie.
  • Warly cooking his unique dishes on multiple Crock Pots.
  • Portable Crock Pot as seen in a PS4 Shipwrecked promotional poster.
  • A Portable Crock Pot as seen in a drawing from Art Stream # 78.

“Now we're cookin'!”
–Wilson
“Just a dumb pot.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang will cook good meal for friends, meal like home!”
–Wolfgang
“For when the hunger gnaws at my tiny stomach...”
–Wendy
“WE ARE KIN, YOU AND I”
–WX-78
“It will be nice to eat some properly prepared food.”
–Wickerbottom
“Sure makes the camp smell nice.”
–Woodie
“An instrument of the dark culinary arts.”
–Maxwell
“Cöoking, a nöble pröfessiön.”
–Wigfrid
“Makes yummies!”
–Webber
“What new culinary adventures shall we undertake, old friend?”
–Warly
“For belly stuff”
–Wormwood
“A fancy pot for some fancy cookin'.”
–Winona
“Does it make soul food?”
–Wortox
“Put food inside and different food come out, florp?”
–Wurt
“It still doesn't beat roasting things over a campfire.”
–Walter
“I don't like to fuss around with fancy cooking.”
–Wanda
The Portable Crock Pot is a character-specific Item that Warly begins the game with. It acts as a Crock Pot that can be moved and replaced. It must be placed on the ground to cook anything.
While cooking, the Portable Crock Pot gives just enough light to ward off Charlie, but the sanity drain is equivalent of staying in total darkness, therefore, this is not recommended. It can also make exclusive recipes.

Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Portable Crockpot was exclusive to the Shipwrecked DLC, until it was introduced to Don't Starve Together in the Warly Character Update.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
In Shipwrecked, if the player places a Portable Crock Pot inside a Cargo Boat or Chester, exits the game and then relogs, a second Portable Crock Pot will appear in the player's inventory as the game considers the original one to be missing.
The same bug will occur when the player enters a Cave without the Portable Crock Pot in the inventory. Relogging is not necessary for this to occur.
